architecture_fit.note: Single QK head: one-hot nesting-level match + recency tiebreak, a minimal delta from base_model self-attention, no MLP, solves the stack-pop routing exactly.

baseline_comparison.note: Depth-sweep bars contrast the stack head against nearest-opener (collapses with depth) and random (floor) under identical conditions.

faithfulness.note: Synthetic hand-built circuit honestly flagged; the level-removed ablation equals the visualized nearest-opener line, and a model-level patching check is proposed.

operating_range.note: Holds accuracy=1.0 / lift~1.0 across the full depth sweep d1-d5; construction scales but no breaking point is exhibited.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism is hand-set (C, ALPHA, one-hot levels) with no training — exactly the bonus case, reproducing target behaviour.

visual_judgement.note: Heatmap with cyan true-opener rings makes argmax-on-match directly readable; sweep bars make the depth divergence obvious.

visualisation_rationale.note: Why-this-viz section ties heatmap and sweep to the goal's claim — right axes, right baselines, right grain (off-diagonal jumps vs heuristic decay).

notes: A clean, minimal hand-built stack-matching attention head that routes each closer to its parser-pop opener via one-hot nesting-level Q·K plus a recency tiebreak, achieving perfect argmax accuracy and ~1.0 lift over uniform at every depth. Baselines (nearest-opener, random) and the depth sweep make the 'works where the cheap heuristic fails' claim testable and the visualisations are legible. The only gap is the lack of a true causal/model-level ablation, which is inherent to its synthetic nature and honestly disclosed.
